description_long: This object consists of three adjacent, upright gravestones made of weathered stone, each with a pointed arch top and engraved inscriptions. The gravestones vary slightly in height and width, with the central stone being the tallest and widest, while the two flanking stones are shorter and narrower. The surfaces are aged, showing signs of erosion, discoloration, and moss or dirt accumulation near the bases. The inscriptions are partially legible, featuring names, birth and death dates, and epitaphs, typical of 19th-century grave markers. The backs of the stones are plain and unadorned, with a rough, unpolished finish. The overall appearance suggests these gravestones are from a historical cemetery, evoking a somber and respectful atmosphere.
description: A trio of weathered, arched gravestones with engraved inscriptions and aged stone surfaces, arranged side by side. The central stone is the largest, flanked by two smaller, similarly styled markers.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three gravestones with visible inscriptions and weathered stone textures.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the gravestones and their close arrangement.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, unadorned backs of the gravestones with a rough stone finish.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness and alignment of the gravestones.
attribute_tombstone_inscriptions: L.C. JONES BORN Feb. 25, 1822 DIED Dec. 17, 1882
RHODA JONES BORN Feb. 17, 1824 DIED May 8, 1884
